key_quotes: We've gotten a few calls, and those teams know where they stand.

If there's a player that stands out that we feel it's not worth losing, it's not worth even picking up the phone, then we'll just make the pick. But if it's a player that we're not as excited about, or there's a group of players that we would love to pick from, and we can get value for that, then certainly open to listening.
Ultimately, this is a meritocracy, and the best guy will play.
pressers.trade_posture: Staying put at No. 1 very likely; few calls received; value threshold extremely high to move
player_interest_signals: Fernando Mendoza (implied — QB room framing with Cousins/O'Connell)

key_quotes: We're always open. We'll go through this draft process, like I said, and really build the board.
The calls typically don't happen until [the] middle [of the week], as it gets closer to the draft, but we'll always be open and always pick up the phone.
pressers.trade_posture: Open to both move up or back from No. 2; listening posture

key_quotes: I never think it's a good idea to go into a draft and draft for need. I just don't think that's a good philosophy overall.
We're not really naming anybody, and we'll see how that room looks by the time we get [to] August.
pressers.trade_posture: Openly exploring trade-down at No. 3 (engaged with KC); per PHNX, no 'serious' offers yet

key_quotes: Patient, disciplined approach to team-building.
We're in the final stages of preparation.
pressers.trade_posture: Open to trade-down at 4 — BPA oriented

key_quotes: We're going to look for the best player regardless of position, and I'm excited about where the roster is right now based on what we did in free agency.

I've watched a lot of Ohio State defensive film the last couple of months. Those are two of the key players on that defense that we looked at. Certainly guys that we've talked a lot about and think very highly of.
We've gotten a couple calls of teams sniffing around about potentially coming up.
pressers.trade_posture: Willing to move back — multiple teams have called about coming up to 5; now owns 2 top-10 picks via Lawrence trade

key_quotes: We're working through a number of different scenarios at this point.
It's certainly a possibility — there's always someone realistic to trade up for; the key factor is the acquisition cost.
[Carnell Tate] could be a trapeze artist with how acrobatic he is; [Jordyn] Tyson is an excellent creator with the ball in his hands.
Tackles are tackles.
pressers.trade_posture: Not ruling out trade-up; two first-rounders (6, 24) gives ammunition

key_quotes: That's what we wanted to do coming out of free agency: make it so we're not having to pick for need or having to pick, reach for a certain position or player. We feel really good about being able to pick the best player available.
We do what's best for our team.
It's hard to understand or figure out where everything's gonna go. You hear something new every day, and nobody's telling the truth.
pressers.trade_posture: 'More likely than not' staying at No. 7; will NOT trade within NFC East
player_interest_signals: Broad — defense at all three levels

key_quotes: We have flexibility.
We've been scouting a wider variety of prospects heading into this draft.
pressers.trade_posture: Flexibility noted; rebuilding, could move back

key_quotes: It should be an entertaining night. There'll probably be a lot of trades.
I think you're going to get a massive run of offensive lineman from 10-25. I think there are numbers there, but once you get to pick 35, those numbers quickly diminish.
I think our goal is to just make that selection and add a player that is going to be an impact player on our team... I don't want to take a lesser player.
pressers.trade_posture: Actively engaged in trade talks (ARI No. 3 rumored); expects many trades
player_interest_signals: OL run 10-25 signals possible OT target; Rueben Bain EDGE consensus fit

key_quotes: The opportunity to add a player of Dexter's ability was too good to pass up.
Dexter fits the vision we have on our defense and will also elevate others around him.
Excited to turn to the draft and our remaining picks to further enhance our team.
pressers.trade_posture: Just traded FOR No. 10 via Dexter Lawrence deal; likely standing pat and picking

key_quotes: The higher that you take a player, in the first three rounds, you're expecting that guy to be a valuable starter for you.
[De'Von Achane] is not available for trade.
We'll be a draft and develop team.
pressers.trade_posture: Much more likely to trade DOWN than up — need volume (11 picks)

key_quotes: Absolutely. We'll entertain improving or an in-draft read on what gives us a better chance to get another player, and still have our pick and the red meat of top players.
When you've got the kind of assets or the kind of ammunition we've got in this draft, you should look at all machinations.
I've looked at that mirror a lot, about how to go up and down and trade and do those kinds of things.
pressers.trade_posture: Very open to trading — two 1st rounders (12 + 20) from Parsons deal
player_interest_signals: Broad; LB/S hinted via Dillon Thieneman consensus

key_quotes: Our macro philosophy is, if we can use free agency to not be desperate in the draft, we more than likely will be better drafters, because we're not reaching, we're not desperate. We're not going in there with a major void, major hole that has to be filled.
pressers.trade_posture: Disciplined BPA; Snead historically active — poised for 1st-round pick for only 2nd time in a decade

key_quotes: Fano can play wherever you want him to play. He can play tackle, he can play guard.
Vega Ioane — physical, tough. He's probably a guard... prototypes, straight from central casting, of what you want your guards to look like.
[We] finished the third set of draft meetings... happy with the accomplishments.
pressers.trade_posture: 11 picks — trade flexibility acknowledged

key_quotes: You can never go wrong with taking the best player and you are going to be happy at some point that you did instead of trying to force a pick.
We do see that there is a need for help there on the defensive side, for sure.
Our two biggest needs [are] edge rusher and inside linebacker.
pressers.trade_posture: Actively working trade-down scenarios at 15
player_interest_signals: TE strong (Sadiq consensus fit), EDGE/ILB priority

key_quotes: It's a pretty strong draft on the offensive and defensive line. Secondary, maybe not as much. Receiver and tight end are pretty good in comparison to some other recent years.
We could complement our room with some speed. We would like to get faster.
[On Morgan Moses at RT, could be a successor in this class].
pressers.trade_posture: Not explicitly signaled; BPA lean
player_interest_signals: Kayden McDonald (NT) — directly linked in reports

key_quotes: I think really it's us getting to know guys. If you're going to miss on a guy, you're going to miss because you know what he's about from a personal character standpoint.
If anything, [free agency] opens up a lot of possibilities. All positions are on the table.
Ready for all scenarios.
pressers.trade_posture: Ready for all scenarios, including trade-back at 19
player_interest_signals: Pass-rusher, WR for Bryce Young, or top OT

key_quotes: We've homed in on a zone between picks 45 and 75.
[Six players we're focused on at 62 — feel good about those].
[Evan Engram] has a big part to play in 2026.
pressers.trade_posture: All but ruled out trade-up into Round 1 (too costly)

key_quotes: The best player who may be available may be a position that going into the draft we didn't necessarily want to take.
Sometimes when I think about those picks and think about the reasons that I miss on those picks, sometimes I even overcompensate.
[Increasing depth at WR was an offseason priority].
pressers.trade_posture: Roseman 'plotting to shake up' first round (per Schefter); classic misdirection

key_quotes: Love where we are at. Maybe we have 14 [picks].
[There's] no news on Aaron Rodgers.
[Open to picking a player the team did NOT host on a pre-draft visit — breaking trend].
pressers.trade_posture: Trade-active — 12 picks, could become 10-14 depending on maneuvering

key_quotes: I have made zero calls about Quentin, and I've had zero calls regarding Quentin.
We're really excited about the wide receiver room.
We have players coming up that are going to be eating up a lot of that cap room.
pressers.trade_posture: Many options — limited disclosure per reports

key_quotes: It's about winning now.
If there's players who we can sign that give us the opportunity to win now more so than holding out for a compensatory pick in 2027, that's the decision we're going to make.
The most important thing is our ability to win in 2026.
pressers.trade_posture: No 2026/2027 Round 1 pick (Parsons trade); first pick at 52 — open to trading up

key_quotes: It's no secret with us. We have four picks, so we'll be looking to move back.
I think there's a lack of depth in this draft.
We've talked within our division... Everybody in our division we would trade with, and we have good relationships with all three of those teams.
pressers.trade_posture: Publicly committed to trading BACK from 32 — premium targeted

key_quotes: [On 'eff them picks' mentality for a Super Bowl push] Absolutely, yeah.
[Draft class is] very similar to the past couple drafts.
[Drafting 24-25 year-olds raises questions about longevity into second contract].
pressers.trade_posture: Willing to be bold/trade up

key_quotes: You probably don't get as many consensus names anywhere near getting to 26.
There are areas in this draft where you can find players to help your team... incrementally help our roster, whether it's depth, starter, special teams or whatever it may be.
You know I'll always listen.
pressers.trade_posture: Always listening; fewer Round 1 grades this year — could move

key_quotes: The more shots you have at the board, the better.
[Likes] the depth in this year's class at linebacker, edge rusher, wide receiver, interior offensive line and safety.
pressers.trade_posture: Expects to be 'active' up or back; no Round 1 pick
player_interest_signals: None; Rounds 3-5 is his sweet spot

key_quotes: We'll see if we pick — if we pick, we pick; if we don't, we don't. I'm sure we'll keep you on your toes like we always do.
If we move out of the first round, I'll probably get some choice words about the draft party.
We'll take the best football players, regardless of the position.
pressers.trade_posture: Weighing trade OUT of Round 1

key_quotes: [Deepest groups are] cornerback, safety and defensive end.
[Weakest groups relative to past drafts are] running backs and defensive tackles.
[On WR class]: 'Receivers are deep.'
pressers.trade_posture: No Round 1 pick, first on Day 2; limited capital (5 picks)

key_quotes: You're always going to stay agile.
[On Travis Hunter]: 'For all intents and purposes, he'll be a limited participant through the offseason program with eyes on return to play at full tick in training camp.'
[Coen]: 'It's just allowed us to maybe get to different or deeper dialogue quicker on some of the guys we're really honing in on.'
pressers.trade_posture: Agile — could move around; first pick No. 56 overall

key_quotes: Ready to go here in a few days and per usual, really happy with our entire organization.
The thing I probably love most about the Draft is that it involves every ounce of our organization.
pressers.trade_posture: Standard posture — willing to move

key_quotes: [Interim leadership after Adofo-Mensah departure — 'collaborative' approach].

key_quotes: [We want] sustainable success, which starts with saving money by nailing draft picks.

high_confidence_pick_locks.notes: Spytek presser + every major final mock converge. Only scenario breaking this is a draft-day trade, for which Spytek set a very high value bar.
Player is near-lock IF ARI stays — but Ossenfort openly shopping pick with KC. Bailey could slide to 9.
Schrager & McShay dissent (Bailey/Mauigoa at 2). Reese still 4+ analyst lock.

top_surprise_candidates: Schrager 'torn up' prior mock after teams told him certain picks weren't happening — biggest unknown is which picks shifted
Browns at 6 — Kiper/Brugler/Consensus go Tate/Downs, but B/R has Kadyn Proctor (OT) as the SURPRISE; Berry publicly praised Tate AND Tyson
Washington at 7 — McShay & Simms now projecting a TRADE-DOWN/UP swap, directly contradicting Peters' 'more likely than not staying' presser statement. This is a live signal-shift.
Miami at 11 — Sullivan publicly said 'much more likely to trade DOWN' — multiple mocks still have MIA picking OL/DL, but a move to late teens is plausible
Jerry Jones at 12 — self-describing as wanting to 'go up and down and trade'; Cowboys with two 1sts are the most likely trade-up catalyst
Final mocks are now converging on Jordyn Tyson (ASU) at 18-20 despite prior consensus for Makai Lemon being WR2 — Tyson +3 spots on Jeremiah's final big board
Jermod McCoy (CB, TN) slipping out of top 10 in Kiper + Jeremiah finals due to medical concerns; consensus only 9% at slot 10. Could land 11-20.
Aaron Rodgers decision still pending as of 4/20 Khan presser — if he signs with PIT, 21st pick shifts from QB contingency to BPA

signal_shifts_vs_earlier_mocks.detail: Jeremiah bumped Downs 8→7; Brugler/Kiper go Downs at 5. Safety class thinner than expected.
Jeremiah moved him 15→19 on final big board; medical drives consensus at slot 10 down to 9%
+3 to No. 18 on Jeremiah's final; Schrager slotting Tyson as WR1 off board at 9. New top-15 candidate.
+6 spots on Jeremiah's final big board; now top-40.

Veach: 'there'll probably be a lot of trades.' Jerry Jones 'absolutely' open. Seattle public about moving back. Miami public about moving back. ARI shopping 3. This is the most trade-expected R1 in recent memory.

team_agent_update_suggestions.update: Peters explicitly said 'more likely than not staying at 7' and 'we will NOT trade in NFC East'. Tighten trade-posture to 'low-probability trade-out, zero intra-NFC-East trades'. Agent should prefer BPA defender.
team_agent_update_suggestions.contradiction_note: McShay and Simms final mocks BOTH project a WAS trade-down. Analyst feedback should not override direct GM statement — keep Peters' stated posture as primary, analyst content as secondary.

team_agent_update_suggestions.update: Spytek framing Mendoza selection as near-certainty; trade threshold explicitly very high. Lock Raiders at Mendoza in agent decision logic with ~0.97 probability.
Sullivan said 'much more likely to trade down' — increase MIA trade-down willingness. First three rounds = 'valuable starter' expectation, which favors DL/OL profile.
Schneider PUBLIC about moving back from 32 AND willing to trade within division. Elevate SEA trade-back probability significantly, include NFC West partners.
Ossenfort confirmed engaged trade discussions with KC; 'not really naming anybody' at QB + hinting at Ty Simpson Round 2. Add Ty Simpson as R2 target, open trade-down from 3.
Khan: may break 'Round 1 = visited player' trend. Loosen Steelers' historical visit-correlation prior.
Wolf: 'would like to get faster' at EDGE + direct Kayden McDonald links. Elevate NT/interior-DL + speed EDGE targets.
Jerry Jones 'absolutely' trading-active. Two 1sts + public willingness = highest trade-activity probability in Round 1.
Schoen specifically watched lot of Ohio State defensive film — Styles and Downs explicitly named. Downweight WR/OT fits at 5.
Berry praised Tate AND Tyson as WR; 'tackles are tackles' — flexible at 6. Open to trade-up too.
Veach signaled massive OL run 10-25 — KC is likely the upward-mobile team (rumored ARI #3 partner) or takes OL at 23 if available.
Licht public about trade-down from 15 + EDGE/ILB as top-2 needs. TE Sadiq still in play but trade-down risk high.
Morgan 'all positions on the table, ready for all scenarios' at 19 — keep broad posture.
